title: cephalocarpus humilis
intro: Cephalocarpus humilis, also known as the lesser club moss or lycopodiopsida, is a small, perennial plant that belongs to the club moss family and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. Traditionally, Cephalocarpus humilis has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cephalocarpus humilis are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and skin infections. Cephalocarpus humilis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
